content: TRENDING REGIME DETECTION: ADX > 25 and rising. Price above 50/100/200 SMA. Bollinger bands expanding. RSI between 40-80. Sequential MA stack (10>20>50>100>200 SMA) = Perfect Order. Enter 5 candles after formation. Exit when 10 SMA crosses below 20. Crypto: use 50/200 EMA crossover on daily as primary trend filter.

RANGING REGIME DETECTION: ADX < 25 trending down. Oscillating RSI 30-70. Contracting Bollinger Bands. Price oscillating between S/R. Fader strategy: ADX < 35, wait for 15-pip break below prior day low, enter long 15 pips above prior day high. Stop 30 pips, target 60 pips. Never pick tops in trends or buy breakouts in ranges.

REGIME TRANSITION DETECTION: Three regimes: (1) Trending with momentumâ€”follow trend. (2) Range-bound mean reversionâ€”fade extremes. (3) Transitioningâ€”reduce size 50%. Identify by ADX direction change, Bollinger bandwidth expansion/contraction, correlation stability. Corrections being bought = near-equilibrium (trend intact). Bounces being sold = far-from-equilibrium (regime change).

DONCHIAN BREAKOUT SYSTEM: Enter on 20-day channel breakout. Exit on 10-day breakout in opposite direction. Add 350/25-day EMA trend filter: longs only when 25-EMA > 350-EMA. Stop at 2x ATR. Trend filter improves edge ratio from 1.20 to 1.35+ by filtering counter-trend false breakouts.

VOLATILITY BREAKOUT ENTRY: Enter when price moves > N*ATR from prior close. If close > open + N*ATR, buy. Filter with ADX > 25. In crypto use 1.5-2x ATR(14) for breakout threshold. Combine with volume surge for highest conviction.

HIGH VOLATILITY REGIME: ATR expanding above N-day average. VIX > 30. Bollinger bands widening. Reduce position inversely with volatility. If ATR doubles, halve position. Crypto: BTC ATR(14) > 5% of price = high vol. Use 1% risk per trade max. Widen stops to 2-3x ATR. Avoid leverage > 3x.

BOLLINGER BAND SQUEEZE: When BB width contracts to multi-month low, explosive move imminent. BB narrow inside Keltner Channels = TTM Squeeze. Crypto: squeezes on 4H/daily precede 15-40% BTC moves. Wait for breakout direction, react. Sell premium (iron condors) while waiting.

CRISIS/CAPITULATION REGIME: MVRV < 0.5. SOPR < 0.85. Exchange outflows surging. Fear and Greed < 15. BTC dominance rising. Funding rates deeply negative. Action: scale into quality with 25% tranches over 2-4 weeks. Wait 48-72h after initial panic. Never catch falling knife in one entry.

EUPHORIA/DISTRIBUTION REGIME: MVRV Z-Score > 7. SOPR > 1.1. Funding > 0.1%/8h sustained. Retail leverage extreme. Scale out 10% per 20% rise above fair value. Buy OTM puts for tail hedging when IV rank < 20%.

TREND EXHAUSTION: Price makes new high but RSI/MACD doesn't = bearish divergence. Strongest after extended trends at key S/R. Fifth wave at 61.8-100% extension + momentum divergence = trend ending. Volume declining on new highs = distribution. OI declining with price = weakening trend.

TREND INITIATION: Golden cross (50 EMA > 200 EMA) daily/weekly. MACD histogram crossing zero on daily + weekly MACD rising. Volume expanding on breakout. New 20-day high with ADX rising through 25. Dual timeframe momentum alignment = highest conviction.

TREND FILTER SYSTEMS: Dual MA: 100/350-day crossover, always in market, CAGR 57.8%. Triple MA: 150/250/350-day, CAGR 48.1%. Random entry + 350/25 EMA trend filter = 32.46% CAGRâ€”proving trend filter alone has massive edge. Entry timing secondary to trading with trend.

MEAN REVERSION DETECTION: Hurst H < 0.5 = mean-reverting. ADF test p < 0.05 = stationary. Half-life via O-U: dP = lambda*(P-mu)+e, half-life = -ln(2)/lambda. If < 10 days, viable. Bollinger z-score: long z < -2, short z > +2, exit at z = 0.
SIMPLE REGIME FILTER: BTC > 200 EMA daily + 50 EMA > 200 EMA = BULL (long only). < 200 + 50 < 200 = BEAR (short/cash). Mixed = reduce 50%. Avoids catastrophic drawdowns in bear markets.

WYCKOFF PHASES: Accumulation = sideways at lows, smart money buying. Markup = trending up. Distribution = sideways at highs, smart money selling. Markdown = trending down. Exchange outflows = accumulation. Inflows = distribution. Large BTC to exchanges = distribution. To cold storage = accumulation.

65-MONTH GLOBAL LIQUIDITY CYCLE: ~65-month debt refinancing cycle is fundamental pacemaker for risk assets. Supersedes Bitcoin halving as primary driver. Expanding = risk assets inflate. Contracting = deflate. Track CB balance sheets, repo markets, shadow bankingâ€”NOT just M2.

DEBT-TO-LIQUIDITY RATIO: Total debt / available refinancing liquidity. Averages ~2x with mean-reverting properties. Below 2x = abundant liquidity = crypto bull. Above 2x = constraints = crypto bear. Single best macro indicator for crypto cycle positioning.

HALVING CYCLE DEGRADATION: 4-year halving cycle superseded by macro forces. ETF inflows, CBDCs, institutional adoption dilute supply-shock dynamics. Weight global debt maturity walls HIGHER than internal blockchain issuance schedules.

DXY INVERSE CORRELATION WITH BTC: Strong inverse over 30+ day periods. DXY is LEADINGâ€”moves before crypto. Break below support = increase crypto exposure. Above resistance = reduce. DXY weighted: EUR 57.6%, JPY 13.6%, GBP 11.9%.

CENTRAL BANK BALANCE SHEET REGIME: CB expansion = liquidity injection = crypto bullish. Track Fed, ECB, BOJ, PBOC weekly. Combined expansion = pump. Contraction = drain. 2020-21 bull: $10T+ expansion. 2022 bear: QT.

ETF FLOWS AS REGIME DRIVER: Sustained inflows > $200M/day = strong bullish. Outflows > $100M/day = bearish. Track daily flows, cumulative holdings vs supply, flow momentum. Delayed 1 dayâ€”use as confirmation.

DEBT REFINANCING CYCLE: 70-80% of capital markets are refinancing. Self-perpetuating until maturity walls hit. Track corporate/sovereign debt maturity schedules, HY credit spreads. Small maturity walls = smooth = abundant liquidity.

SOVEREIGN YIELD REGIME: Rising Treasury yields = higher opportunity cost for non-yielding BTC. Real yields rising = crypto underperforms. Falling = outperforms. Correlation strengthens during macro-driven regimes.

REPO/SHADOW BANKING LIQUIDITY: Global liquidity includes repo + shadow banking (~$200T). Track SOFR spread, commercial paper spreads, cross-currency basis swaps. Stress indicators spike = tightening even if M2 looks fine.

CRISIS ALPHA: Trend followers profited +5% to +82% in Oct 2008. During crisis, trends become more pronounced. Long-option profile benefits from extreme swings. In crypto: systematic short positions during confirmed downtrends. Requires mechanical execution.

INTERMARKET CHAIN: Bonds lead stocks, stocks lead commodities, commodities lead currencies. BTC correlates with risk assets (NASDAQ) during selloffs. Macro events that affect traditional markets now directly impact crypto.

GOLD-DOLLAR INTERMARKET: Correlation -0.6 to -0.8. Both rallying simultaneously = safe haven crisis. Gold rally + BTC rally = digital gold narrative. Gold rally + BTC falling = traditional safe haven preferred.

RISK-ON/RISK-OFF: RISK-ON = stocks up, bonds down, commodities up, USD down. RISK-OFF = opposite. STAGFLATION = stocks down, commodities up. GOLDILOCKS = stocks up, bonds flat. VIX > 30 = fear. AUD/JPY -2%+ = risk-off, sell crypto.

CARRY TRADE REGIME: Borrow JPY/CHF buy AUD/NZD. AUD/JPY tracks risk sentiment. Crypto perp funding = crypto carry. Positive funding > 0.1%/8h sustained = crowded carry. Unwinding triggers cascading liquidations.

YIELD CURVE REGIME: 2Y > 10Y = inversion = recession 6-18 months. Steep = expansion. In crypto: inversion precedes BTC bear markets by 3-6 months. Curve steepening after inversion = early recovery signal.

INTEREST RATE DIFFERENTIAL REGIME: Capital flows to highest yields. Central bank divergence = strongest trend driver. Hawkish Fed = BTC headwind. Dovish pivot = tailwind. Monitor Fed funds futures for rate expectations.
FED POLICY REGIME: Loose policy feeds bubbles. Tightening punctures them. 2020-21 bubble: zero rates + QE. 2022 collapse: tightening. Top data: (1) NFP (2) FOMC (3) CPI (4) Retail Sales (5) PPI.

DOLLAR SMILE THEORY: USD strong when (1) US outperforming or (2) global risk aversion. Weakest in middleâ€”US weak but no crisis. Scenario 1 = moderate BTC headwind. Scenario 2 = strong headwind. Middle = BTC-friendly.

CORRELATION REGIME: Correlations NOT constant. Rolling 50/200-day. Strong break = regime change. In crisis, correlations to 1.0. Crypto: BTC-altcoin > 0.8 during crashes. True diversification = non-crypto assets.
COMMODITY-CURRENCY REGIME: AUD/gold 0.7+. CAD/oil 0.6+. Dollar-commodity: strong negative. BTC increasingly correlated with gold during macro stress.
EQUITY-FX LINKAGE: Rising equities + rising USD = US outperformance. Rising + falling = global risk-on. Falling + rising = risk-off. BTC rising + DXY rising = BTC independent strength (very bullish).
GLOBAL MARKET LEADERSHIP: US leads 1-5 days. Europe 1-3. Asia 1-2. DAX +2% = S&P gaps up. BTC leads alts 1-4 hours. ETH leads DeFi 30-60 min. Use global equity overnight moves as BTC opening bias.
VIX REGIME: < 15 = complacency (sell premium). 15-25 = normal. 25-30 = elevated. > 30 = fear (reduce risk). > 40 = panic (crisis alpha). Contango = bullish. Backwardation = bearish. Flip = defend.

INTERMARKET DIVERGENCE: Gold up miners not = catch-up. S&P up credit widening = correction. Falling yields + rising stocks = goldilocks. Rising yields + falling = tightening. BTC rising + stablecoin dominance rising = defensive despite uptrend.

COT/POSITIONING REGIME: Speculators at 3-year extremes (>90th or <10th percentile) = reversal likely. Crypto equivalent: exchange long/short ratios. Retail 80%+ long = contrarian bearish. 80%+ short = contrarian bullish.

BTC DOMINANCE REGIME: Rising = risk-off within crypto, alts underperform. Falling = alt season. < 40% = late alt mania. > 60% = BTC maximalist. Monitor 7d/30d trend. Only hold alts outperforming BTC on 7d+30d. Exit alts underperforming 2+ weeks.
HALVING CYCLE: 12-18 months pre-halving accumulation, parabolic 6-12 months post, then 80%+ bear. Diminishing returns each cycle. 2013: 100x. 2017: 20x. 2021: 7x. Plan 3-4 year round trips.

MVRV Z-SCORE: > 7 = extreme overvaluation (sell). 3-7 = overvalued. 1-3 = fair. 0-1 = undervalued. < 0 = extreme undervaluation (buy). Historical tops > 7 preceded every major correction. Bottoms < 0 preceded every rally.

SOPR REGIME: > 1.1 = overheated distribution. > 1.0 = in profit. = 1.0 = breakeven (critical). < 1.0 = selling at loss. < 0.98 = capitulation (strong buy). < 0.85 = deep capitulation (aggressive buy). Reset to 1.0 after correction = healthy continuation.

FUNDING RATE REGIME: Normal -0.01% to 0.03%/8h. > 0.1% sustained = extreme long crowding (bearish, squeeze coming). < -0.05% = extreme short crowding (bullish). > 0.1%/8h for 3+ days = reflexive loop max. Unwinding violent.

ALTCOIN SEASON: BTC dominance falling. Alt total cap rising faster than BTC. ETH/BTC rising. Memecoin volume surging. Only hold alts outperforming BTC 7d+30d. Concentrate 3-5 positions. Exit when dominance reverses.

LIQUIDATION CASCADE: BTC drops = liquidation engines sell = death spiral. DeFi auto-liquidations add fuel. Monitor liquidation heatmaps, OI, margin utilization. OI clusters = liquidation magnets. Reduce leverage BEFORE vol spikes. Liquidation vol > 2x daily avg = cascade, wait for dust.

BTC CORRELATION CASCADE: Most cryptos tied to BTC. BTC plummets = all alts follow amplified. Alts = leveraged BTC bet + idiosyncratic risk. Rising dominance = alts underperform. During crashes, all correlations converge to ~1.0.

CRYPTO BUBBLE CYCLES: Diminishing returns each cycle. Faster with more institutional money. 2013: 100x, 2017: 20x, 2021: 7x. Scale out gradually during euphoria. Don't call exact top. Plan 3-4 year round trips.

STABLECOIN REGIME: Rising stablecoin dominance = risk-off. Falling = risk-on. > 10% = defensive. < 5% = fully risk-on. Depeg events create systemic cascading liquidations. Primary crypto risk-on/off gauge.

SECTOR ROTATION: Early = XLF/XLY. Mid = XLK/XLI. Late = XLE/XLB. Recession = XLU/XLP/XLV. RS leads price 1-3 months. Crypto: Early bull = BTC. Mid = ETH/L1s. Late = DeFi/memecoins. Recession = stables/BTC.

MOMENTUM REGIME: Variance Ratio > k = positive autocorrelation (momentum). Time series momentum: buy positive N-month return, sell negative. Cross-sectional: rank by return, buy top decile. Best lookback: 12mo equities, shorter crypto.

ROLL YIELD REGIME: Backwardated (near > far) = trending up. Contango (far > near) = trending down. Crypto perp funding analogous: persistent positive funding = contango (bearish headwind). Negative = backwardation (bullish).

BUBBLE INDICATORS: (1) Rapid price no fundamental change. (2) Media explosion. (3) New era thinking. (4) Inexperienced traders. (5) Derivatives surge. Count = proximity to top. Taxi driver test: tips = late stage 3.
BUBBLE STRUCTURE (SHILLER): (1) Displacement (2) Boom (3) Euphoria (4) Profit-taking (5) Panic. Crypto follows exactly. Each stage compressed vs traditional markets due to 24/7 and social media.

REFLEXIVITY REGIME: Perceptions SHAPE reality. Rising prices = more users = better dev = higher prices (virtuous). Falling = exodus = less dev = lower (vicious). Crypto is MORE reflexive than traditional markets. Corrections bought = near-equilibrium. Bounces sold = far-from-equilibrium.

BOOM-BUST (SOROS): (1) Trend not recognized (2) Survives test (3) Growing conviction (4) Perception-reality divergence (5) Recognition (6) Reversal. Phase 1-3 = trend follow. 4 = reduce. 5-6 = reverse. Bias BECOMES fundamental in crypto.
LEVERAGE SYSTEMIC RISK: When aggregate leverage (OI, funding, margin) reaches extremes, system fragile. One catalyst = cascading liquidations. Max leverage: 3-5x core, 10x highest conviction only.

OI DIVERGENCE: Price new highs + OI declining = weakening (distribution). Price new lows + OI increasing = potential bottom (squeeze building). Combine with funding rate for highest conviction regime signal.

CREDIT/REGULATORY CYCLE: Easy money = leverage = asset inflation = regulators notice = tightening = forced deleveraging = crash. SEC actions, exchange bans puncture bubble as monetary tightening does. Track both.
IMPULSE SYSTEM: 13-EMA slope (trend) + MACD-Histogram slope (momentum). Green/green = long only. Red/red = short only. Mixed = no new positions. Prevents trading against trend-momentum combination.

FOUR DIMENSIONS: (1) Multi-TF momentum (2) Patternâ€”trend vs correction (3) Priceâ€”Fib targets (4) Timeâ€”Fib time retracements. All four align = highest probability. Time-price confluence = max probability reversal zone.

CORRECTION COMPLETION: (1) Price at 38.2-78.6% retracement (2) Time at 38.2-61.8% of prior trend duration (3) Momentum reversal on dual TF (4) ABC complete. All align = enter with larger trend. Wave 4 overlap wave 1 = correction.

CONTRACTION-EXPANSION: Markets alternate contraction/expansion. After contraction expect expansion and vice versa. Monitor BB width and ATR. Tightening = coiled spring. Trade breakout with momentum confirmation. Don't predictâ€”react.

TREND-FILTERED BREAKOUT: Only take breakouts in direction of larger trend. 20-day breakout during weekly uptrend = high prob. During downtrend = bear trap. Multi-TF alignment dramatically improves success. E-ratio improves 1.20 to 1.35+.

OSCILLATOR REGIME (RANGING ONLY): RSI < 30 = oversold buy. > 70 = overbought sell. Stochastic %K crosses %D in oversold. CCI < -100 buy, > +100 sell. ADX < 25 confirms range. Divergences at extremes strongest. In trending markets oscillators stay extended.

IMPLIED VOLATILITY REGIME: IV rank < 20% = complacency, buy vol. > 80% = panic, sell vol. Crypto IV floor ~50% annualized, spikes to 150%+. Mean-reversion most reliable pattern. IV > RV = overpriced sell. IV < RV = underpriced buy.
VOLATILITY SURFACE: Crypto extreme skewâ€”put IV >> call IV (crash fear premium). Puts overpriced relative to calls but tail risk real. Gamma explodes ATM in final 48h. Close/roll at 21 DTE.

DRAWDOWN REGIME: 30-50% drawdowns NORMAL for trend following. PTJ: 3% monthly stop if down. Never double-digit monthly loss. Predefine max DD, size positions within tolerance. Never abandon system during drawdownâ€”biggest trends follow biggest pain.
FEAR CONTAGION: Fear spreads faster than greed. One exchange collapse = panic across ALL crypto. Buy fundamentally sound assets at irrational discounts, but ONLY after 48-72h minimum.

NARRATIVE REGIME: Prices driven by stories. BTC narratives shift: digital gold = inflation hedge = institutional = national reserve. When narrative exhausts marginal buyer, price stagnates. Recycling old narratives = mature cycle.

CBDC IMPACT: May reduce stablecoin demand. Enable programmable money for regulation. May increase overall digital adoption. Short-term neutral to slightly bearish stables. Long-term neutralâ€”different purposes.

LTCM LEVERAGE LESSON: 25:1 leverage on market-neutral. When correlations broke, losses 10x capital. Crypto offers 100x. Max: 3-5x core, 10x max conviction. Models on 2-3yr data fail spectacularly during regime changes.

ERGODICITY: Ensemble average != time average. One bad sequence = ruin = eliminated. Survival > optimization. 50% WR 1:1 R:R, 2% risk = near-zero ruin. 10% risk = 99% ruin. Always preserve ability to keep playing.

TAIL RISK HEDGING: Deep OTM puts cheap during calm, pay 10-50x during crashes. Buy when IV rank < 20%. Most expire worthlessâ€”few that pay cover all costs. In crypto, once-in-lifetime events happen quarterly.

TURTLE SYSTEM: S1: 20-day breakout, 10-day exit, skip if last winner. S2: 55-day, 20-day exit, no filter. Size: (Account x 1%) / (N x Point Value). Pyramid 0.5N, max 4 units. Stop 2N. Max 2% per market. Crypto: shorter periods (10-day/5-day).
REGIME-ADAPTIVE SIZING: Kelly f* = (p*b-q)/b. Use half-Kelly. ATR-based: (Account x Risk%) / (N x Point Value). High-vol: 1% max. Low-vol: 2% OK. Size by volatility, never by dollar conviction.

PYRAMIDING: Add at 0.5N intervals. Max 4 units. Each unit stop = 2N from ITS entry. Earlier stops to breakeven after 2nd unit. Total max risk 2% per market. Livermore: start 25%, add 25% each confirmation. NEVER add to losers.

TRAILING STOP: ATR-based: exit when price retraces N*ATR from high. Start wider (3*ATR), tighten as trade matures. Two-day low stop for longs. At 2x risk profit, half closed, rest to breakeven. Trail with parabolic SAR or 2-bar low.
TIME-BASED EXIT: Exit after N bars regardless of P/L. Donchian with 80-day time exit outperformed breakout exit. Captures trending portion, avoids consolidation. Loss after 1-2 weeks = clearly wrong.
RANDOM ENTRY TEST: Random entries + SES exit (3*ATR stop + 6*ATR target + 10 bar time) profited on S&P. Proves exit strategy can be MORE important than entry.

CRISIS EVENT: When something disturbs emotional equilibrium, close ALL related positions. Kovner: don't lose great deal for reasons you don't understand. Regulatory surprises, exchange collapses, protocol hacks = immediate risk reduction.

CONTRARIAN ENTRY: Go against hysteriaâ€”95% of time profitable if you stick it out. But examine fully first. Maximum pessimism = best buying. Maximum optimism = best selling. Requires predefined entry criteria.
EQUITY CURVE FILTER: Equity trend down = cut back. Losing faster than making = warning. Equity below own 20-day MA = reduce size 50%. Above = trade normally. Prevents compounding errors.

TAYLOR RULE: Optimal rate = neutral + 0.5*(inflation-target) + 0.5*(output gap). Significant deviation = expect policy shift. Fed behind curve = inflationary, mixed for crypto. Ahead = restrictive, bearish.
PPP REGIME: Currencies revert to PPP long-term. Apply to BTC via MVRV/NVT. When price exceeds fundamental models > 2 sigma, mean reversion likely 6-18 months.

TRADE FLOW REGIME: Net exporters = currency appreciation. BTC equivalent: exchange outflows = accumulation (bullish). Inflows = distribution (bearish). Large wallet movements to exchanges precede selling 24-72h.

ECONOMIC SURPRISE: Data beats expectations = currency strengthens. Reversion to mean after extremes. Positive surprises = risk-on = BTC tailwind. Negative = headwind. Price diverges 1+ month from fundamentals = violent correction.
CAPITAL FLOW: ETF flows = institutional TIC data for crypto. Sustained > $200M/day = conviction building. Outflows > $100M/day = institutional retreat. Equity rallies attract foreign capital.
MONEY SUPPLY REGIME: M2 > GDP growth = inflationary. Expanding M2 = BTC tailwind. Contracting = headwind. Track global M2 (not just US). M2 turning positive after contraction = early bull signal.
SEASONALITY: Q4 historically strongest. Q1 post-halving strongest. Summer weaker. Monday CME gap fills ~70%. Month-end rebalancing flows. Use as filter, not primary signal.
DUAL TF MOMENTUM: Daily + weekly both show reversal in same direction. Weekly MACD histogram rising + daily crossing zero = highest conviction. Daily alone = false signals. Weekly alone = too slow.

NEWS SENTIMENT MOMENTUM: NLP sentiment scores predict short-term momentum. Decay is fastâ€”hold days. Crypto: CT/Discord sentiment + Google Trends. Extreme sentiment + key technical level = highest conviction.

INTEREST RATE PARITY: Forward = Spot x (1+domestic)/(1+foreign). Widening spreads = trend acceleration. Crypto funding rate = crypto interest rate. Persistent positive = elevated. Converging to zero = regime shift.

COINTEGRATION REGIME: Non-stationary series with stationary spread. ADF on residuals p < 0.05. Better than correlationâ€”predictable spread with low correlation. In crypto: ETH/BTC, SOL/ETH cointegration breaks during regime changes.

KALMAN FILTER: Dynamic hedge ratio adapting to changing relationships. Auto-adjusts positions as relationship drifts. Detects regime changes in cointegration. Hedge ratio diverging sharply from historical = regime change.

REGIME SHIFT IN BACKTESTING: Predictive power fails when structure changes. 2001: decimalization. 2008: crisis. Crypto: each cycle structurally different. Ask: Has structure changed since backtest? Don't assume patterns repeat.
OVERFITTING: Curve-fitting #1 system killer. Develop on multiple instruments. Walk-forward: if validation degrades >30% = overfit. Accept if out-of-sample Sharpe > 50% of in-sample.

MONTE CARLO VALIDATION: Randomize trade order 1000+ times. Worst 5% = realistic worst case. Crypto: use fat-tailed (Student-t) not normal. >95% simulations profitable = real edge. 60% = could be luck.

MULTI-SYSTEM REGIME: Trend following + mean reversion combined: 23-37% annual, 6-13% max DD. Low correlation between strategies. In crypto: combine trend + mean reversion + carry (funding) for all-weather.

MULTI-FACTOR SCORING: Score each bar on trend alignment, momentum, volatility, volume. Only enter above threshold. Outperforms single-indicator entries. Crypto: combine EMA, RSI, ATR, volume into composite score.

CPPI: Maintain floor value. Risk = 2*(equity - floor). During drawdowns auto-reduces exposure. Crypto: floor at 70% of peak. At floor = 0% risk. At peak = max risk. More robust than fixed Kelly during crashes.

SESSION REGIME: Asian: quiet, zone building. London: breakouts. US-Europe overlap: 70% of range. Crypto: BTC vol peaks US equity hours (9:30AM-4PM EST). Weekend = thin liquidity. Align strategies with sessions.

FIBONACCI CONFLUENCE: Multiple Fib levels from different swings converging = extremely significant zone. Strong trends: 38.2-61.8% pullbacks. 0.618 most common. 0.382 = shallow (strong). 0.786 = deep (weak). Confluence with S/R + volume profile = high-probability.
FIBONACCI TIME: Apply Fib ratios to TIME. Project correction at 38.2%, 50%, 61.8% of prior trend duration. Price target + time target simultaneously = highest probability reversal zone.
CME GAP REGIME: BTC CME gaps fill ~70%. Monday gaps reveal institutional sentiment. Large trend-direction gaps = continuation. Use gap levels as magnet/mean-reversion targets for spot.

OPTIONS EXPIRY: Large expiries (>$1B) create pin risk at max-pain. MM delta-hedging creates buying/selling pressure. Week before: price gravitates to max-pain. Post-expiry: directional move. Deribit: 08:00 UTC Friday.

VOLUME REGIME: Breakouts on low volume = suspect. Reversals on high volume = significant. Volume is most honest indicator in cryptoâ€”harder to fake. Force Index = Price Change x Volume. Monitor exchange AND on-chain volume separately.

GOOGLE TRENDS: Search volume peaks at cycle tops. When crypto terms exceed previous cycle highs = late-stage mania. Attention cascade: rising prices = attention = buying = media = more buying. Leading indicator of sentiment exhaustion.
MARKET MEMORY: Old S/R zones work betterâ€”market has memory. Round numbers ($50K, $100K) + options strikes = meaningful levels. Price accelerates through or bounces sharply. No middle ground.
MA SLOPE REGIME: Increasing = accelerating. Decreasing = decelerating. Flat = ranging. Slope direction change = early warning of phase change. 100-bar MA on 4H/daily defines trend. Above = buys only.

REAL RATES: Real rates (nominal - inflation) drive capital flows. Highest real rates attract capital. Falling real rates = BTC tailwind. Rising = headwind. Negative real rates strengthen BTC inflation-hedge narrative.
MULTI-UNIT EXIT: 1st target 1:1 R/R (breakeven). 2nd at prior swing or 2:1. 3rd trailing. Scaling out: 1/3 at 1x risk, 1/3 at 2x, final 1/3 trailing. Ensures profit while allowing runners.

WAVE 5 COMPLETION: Project at 61.8-100% extension of waves 1-3. + momentum divergence = trend completion. Wave 3 longest. Wave 4 never overlaps Wave 1. Wave 5 shows momentum divergence. ABC retrace 38.2-61.8%.

PRICE ACTION: Big Shadow (engulfing at zone) = buy/sell signal. Kangaroo Tail (long-wick at zone, small body) = rejection. Longer wick relative to body = stronger. Especially significant on 4H/daily at round numbers.

TRIANGLE REGIME: Ascending = bullish continuation. Descending = bearish. Symmetrical = direction from origin trend. Ascending triangles before halving historically resolve upward. Target = height from breakout.
WEDGE REGIME: Rising wedge = bearish (20-40% BTC corrections). Volume decline during formation. Falling wedge = bullish, marks cycle bottoms. Volume expand on breakout.

HEAD AND SHOULDERS: Left shoulder = head = right shoulder. Break below neckline = reversal. Target = head-to-neckline projected below. Volume decrease on right shoulder. Very reliable on BTC/ETH daily/weekly.
CUP AND HANDLE: U-shaped bottom + short handle = bullish. Enter above handle. Target = cup depth projected above. Handle on declining volume, breakout on surge. Reliable on large-cap crypto.

DOUBLE TOP/BOTTOM: Test resistance/support twice and fail. At round numbers ($50K, $100K) especially significant due to psychology + options strikes. Triple bottom = stronger confirmation. Volume decline on second test.

DIAMOND REVERSAL: Signals trend reversal at tops/bottoms. Calculate breakout level as diamond height projected below break. Enter short when price drops below projected level. High volume confirmation. Excellent on BTC 4H/daily during distribution.
EMA BOUNCE: Buy pullbacks to 20 EMA in uptrend. ADX > 25 confirms trend. MA Ribbon (10/20/50 EMA): all aligned = strong. When ribbon flattens = trend ending. Enter on pullback to 20 EMA.

NEWS EVENT: Don't trade 30min before/15min after NFP/FOMC. Initial spike often reversed. Secondary move is real. Boomerang: wait 50-61.8% retracement of spike, enter original direction. Stop below 76.4%.
TREND RETRACEMENT: After breakout, pullback to 38.2-50% = low-risk re-entry. Volume decrease on pullback = healthy. Increase = distribution. 38.2-61.8% = sweet spot for continuation entries.
SWING STRUCTURE: Uptrend = HH + HL sequence. Enter on break of swing high. Trail using swing points. Sequence break (LH in uptrend, HL in downtrend) = psychological foundation shift.

BOND-STOCK DIVERGENCE: Falling yields + rising stocks = goldilocks (crypto friendly). Rising + falling = tightening (headwind). Yields lead stocks 1-3 months. Rising yields + BTC rising = independent strength (very bullish).
OIL-STOCK CORRELATION: Fluctuates -0.5 to +0.5. Break from norm = regime change. Rising oil + falling stocks = stagflation. Oil spikes causing inflation fears = BTC headwind.

PUMP AND DUMP: (1) Unexplained volume surge (2) Social media hype (3) Price +500% in hours (4) Insiders sell (5) Price -90% in hours. If not in before pump, NEVER chase. Pattern from 1700s South Sea Bubble.

BTC AS LEADING INDICATOR: BTC led NASDAQ by 2-3 months in recent cycles. BTC/SPX ratio = macro divergence indicator. BTC breakout while equities lag = risk-on leading. BTC breakdown while equities hold = early warning.

BREADTH REGIME: Track altcoins making new 30-day highs vs lows. NH-NL negative while BTC new highs = internal weakness, distribution. NH-NL positive during BTC lows = accumulation, reversal. Breadth leads price.
EXPECTANCY: (Win% x Avg Win) - (Loss% x Avg Loss). Must be positive including ALL costs. 30% WR + 5:1 R:R > 70% WR + 1:1. Backtest with 10-50 bps crypto slippage. Net < 0 = don't trade.
WALK-FORWARD: Optimize in-sample, test out-of-sample. Walk forward. If validation degrades >30% = overfit. Live Sharpe > 50% backtest Sharpe = viable. 70% train, 15% validation, 15% test.

FALSE BREAKOUT: 20-day high = 2-day low within 3 days = new 20-day high within 3 more days. Enter on re-break. Flushes weak hands. Crypto: false breakouts at round numbers common. Wait for re-break confirmation.

CHANNEL BREAKOUT: Parallel trendlines containing price. Enter on breakout above upper line. Stop below channel. Crypto: ascending channels in bulls, descending in bears. Volume confirmation essential.
INTERMARKET MOMENTUM DRIVERS: (1) Roll returns (2) Forced buying/selling (3) Herding (4) Information diffusion. Each suggests different timeframes. Combine all four for robust momentum signal.

CROSS-SECTIONAL MEAN REVERSION: Short top performers, long bottom in same sector. Rebalance weekly/monthly. Market-neutral. In crypto: rotate from top alts to lagging quality monthly. Fails in strong trends.
LEVERAGED ETF DECAY: 3x daily rebalancing: +10% then -10% = -1% underlying, -9% leveraged. Never hold > few days. Short both bull + bear to capture decay. Crypto leveraged tokens same issue.
VIX FUTURES: Almost always contango. Short front month, roll before expiry. Contango inverts during spikes = go long. VX can spike 300%+. Apply to BTC vol products.

MARKET EFFICIENCY: Crypto less efficientâ€”fewer institutions, 24/7, info asymmetry, behavioral biases. Exploitable but shrinking. Simple momentum still works on BTC weekly. Complex systems don't outperform simple.
SYSTEM ROBUSTNESS: Use broad parameter ranges (20-40 day breakout) not one optimal (23 days). Same parameters across all markets. Simple broadly-parameterized systems most robust.
TIME BAND: Fib time projections from multiple swing points create time bands. Price at Fib price level within time band = maximum probability reversal zone.
CORRELATION-AWARE LIMITS: Don't max positions in BTC/ETH/SOL/AVAXâ€”all >0.8 correlated. Check pairwise. >0.7 = one position for sizing. Max 4/market, 12/correlated group, 10/direction, 20 total.
MAGAZINE COVER: Mainstream media featuring crypto trend = near exhaustion. Time/Bloomberg Bitcoin covers mark cycle tops. Contrarian at extremes. Combine with narrative exhaustion.
RETAIL POSITIONING: 80%+ long = contrarian bearish. 80%+ short = contrarian bullish. CT unanimous bullish = danger. Everyone terrified = opportunity. Crowd right during trends, wrong at extremes.

SYSTEMIC INTERCONNECTION: Terra/Luna = 3AC = Celsius = Voyager cascade. One failure cascades. Diversify across protocols/exchanges. When systemic risk elevated, leverage to 0, increase stables. Never >20% single convergence trade.
VALUATION ANCHORING: Crypto investors anchor to round numbers or previous highs. MVRV Z-Score, NVT, S2F provide pseudo-fundamental anchors. >2 sigma deviation = mean-reversion likely 6-18 months.
SCENARIO PLANNING: Create scenarios weekly. Define expected ranges and actions if broken. Predetermined plansâ€”no heat-of-moment decisions. Imagine alternatives, test until market confirms one.
WEEKEND CLOSE: Strong Friday close at yearly highs = significant. Don't hold against trend direction. CME gaps fill ~70%. Weekend BTC moves preview Monday institutional sentiment.

REGULATORY DISRUPTION: SEC actions, exchange bans, protocol failures = exogenous regime disruption. NOT predictable by TA. Reduce all positions immediately. Reassess 48-72h. Re-enter when clarity emerges.

RS DIVERGENCE: Pairs making new highs while others fail = potential reversal. ETH/BTC rising + BTC stagnant = alt rotation. ETH/BTC falling + BTC rising = BTC quality flight. SOL/ETH shows L1 competition.
TOP-DOWN: (1) BTC > 200 EMA (2) Sector RS: L1s vs DeFi vs memes (3) Best RS token in sector (4) Time with BTC trend. Monthly: rank 3-mo RS, long top 3, rebalance.
GAMMA RISK: Explodes ATM in final 48h. Small moves = massive delta swings. Close/roll at 21 DTE. Deribit 08:00 UTC Friday expiry. Pin risk and gamma squeezes common.
SYNTHETIC POSITION: Long call + short put = synthetic long spot. Avoids perp funding bleed (0.1%+/8h in euphoria). Use when funding expensive and IV skew moderate.
COVERED CALL: Sell OTM calls against long BTC/ETH. Best during ranging with elevated IV. Yield 1-5% monthly on Deribit during high IV. Close at 50% profit.

VOLATILITY SELLING: Crypto IV structurally elevated (50-100%). Persistent premium-selling opportunities. Cash-secured puts, iron condors, covered calls. Risk: black swan. Size for 3 sigma. Never sell naked.
IRON CONDOR: Sell OTM put + call spreads. Close at 50% or 2x credit stop. Best IV rank > 50 + range-bound. ADX < 25, BB contracting. BTC condors yield 2-5% monthly during consolidation.
STRADDLE EVENT: Buy ATM call + put before major events. Buy when IV rank < 30%, sell > 70%. Crypto event-driven vol makes straddles profitable. Best 30-45 DTE.
PROTECTIVE PUT: Buy OTM puts as insurance. Best when IV rank < 20% (complacency). Avoid when IV extreme (overpaying). Collar = sell call to finance put. Deep OTM puts as tail hedge during calm.
CALENDAR SPREAD: Sell near-term, buy longer-term same strike. Profits from theta differential and/or rising IV. Best ranging + stable/rising IV. Risk: large directional move.
SURVIVORSHIP BIAS: Backtesting only surviving chains ignores hundreds that went to zero. Results biased upward 30-50%+. Include dead coins. Ask: what happened to those who failed?
OBJECTIVE TA ONLY: Subjective TA has no statistical evidence. Objective (MA crossovers, momentum) can be backtested. For algo crypto: ONLY quantifiable signals. If can't code it, it's an opinion.
LOSING STREAK RESPONSE: After devastating loss, shrink to 1/5-1/10 position. Get any profit to rebuild confidence. Biggest losses follow largest profits. After strong run, play smaller. Take day off.
UNDERTRADE: Cut position at least in half from what you think. Novices trade 3-5x too big. Max 3-5 trades/day. Quality 2-3x in first 2 hours. Overtrading is #1 killer. Set max trades/week (3-5).
STAGNATION EXIT: Loss after 1-2 weeks = clearly wrong. Even breakeven with time passed = probably wrong. BTC position flat 2+ weeks during expected trend = exit. Capital has opportunity cost.

BIG MOVE POSITIONING: 95% of profits from 5% of trades. Missing few major opportunities has dramatic negative impact. Crypto big moves (50-200% BTC, 1000%+ alts) make the year. 1-3 trades/year generate most profits.
FUNDAMENTAL-TECHNICAL CONFLUENCE: Best trades have ALL THREE: (1) Fundamentals suggest imbalance (2) Chart confirms (3) Market tone correctâ€”bull shrugs off bearish news. Restrict to these setups.

REGIME ADAPTATION: Each crypto cycle structurally different. Soros: markets as historical processes, not equilibrium. Each event changes system permanently. Don't assume previous patterns repeat. Adapt to current structure.
DON'T PREDICT, REACT: System doesn't predictâ€”it reacts to breakouts. Stop calling tops/bottoms. Set alerts, execute when triggered. The system is always right. You are the variable.

MACRO CORRELATION TRACKING: BTC correlated with NASDAQ during selloffs. Monitor BTC/SPX, BTC/QQQ rolling correlation. Correlation breaks = crypto-specific bullish. Spike to 1.0 during selloff = full risk-off.

HALVING vs MACRO: Weight global debt maturity walls and CB balance sheets HIGHER than blockchain issuance. ETF flows + institutional = halving shock diluted. When halving conflicts with macro, follow macro.
ENTRY POINT IRRELEVANCE: Think of entry as last night's close. Only question: bullish or bearish TODAY? If wouldn't enter new position at current price, exit existing one regardless of cost basis.

CATALYST REQUIREMENT: Bottom fishing = expensive gambling. Bottoming markets can go nowhere for years without catalyst. Don't buy just because cheap. Require catalyst: upgrade, ETF, halving, macro pivot.
HARDIEST METHOD: Look for robust, not optimum. System working on BTC/ETH/SOL + traditional markets more robust than one tuned to just BTC. Combine multiple systems across markets.
FACTOR MOMENTUM: Buy instruments with positive N-month return, sell negative. Hold 1 month. Cross-sectional: rank by return, buy top decile, sell bottom. Best lookback: 12mo equities, shorter crypto.

PSYCHOLOGY REGIME: Trading system = 10% of success. Psychology and risk management = 90%. Simple systems robust; complex systems break. Emotional discipline > analytical brilliance. Boring is profitable.
PLAY GREAT DEFENSE: Every day assume every position is wrong. Know stop risk points. Define max possible drawdown. Spend rest of day enjoying positions going your way. PTJ's core principle.

SITTING TIGHT: It was never my thinking that made big money. It was always my sitting. Men who can be right AND sit tight are uncommon. In crypto: the big trend is your edge. Don't scalp small profits when positioned for the big move.
CROWD PSYCHOLOGY: When you join crowd, IQ drops. CT/Discord = crowd amplification machines. Crowd euphoric = wants you to buy at top. Terrified = sell at bottom. Use crowd as CONTRARY indicator.
CUT LOSERS, RIDE WINNERS: Losses 2-2.5x more painful than equivalent gains. Leads to holding losers too long and cutting winners. Counter: automate exits. Remove emotional decision-making entirely.
DON'T BE A HERO: Don't have ego. Always question yourself. Second you feel very good, you are dead. PTJ's biggest hits came after great periods when he started thinking he knew something.

FORK EVENTS AS REGIME SIGNALS: Hard forks create new chains/tokens. Pre-fork: accumulate for airdrop. Post-fork: sell weaker chain quickly as it typically dumps. Monitor GitHub for fork signals and community sentiment splits. Contentious splits = uncertainty = reduce exposure until clarity.

ALTCOIN CLASSIFICATION BY REGIME: Layer-1s (ETH, SOL, AVAX) compete on smart contracts. Layer-2s (MATIC, ARB) scale L1s. DeFi tokens (UNI, AAVE) power protocols. Memecoins trade on narrative alone. Each category has different risk/reward and BTC correlation. Diversify across categories. Memecoins = late-stage bull indicator.

51% ATTACK RISK ASSESSMENT: When miners control >50% of hashing power can manipulate transactions. Smaller PoW chains vulnerable. Monitor hash rate distribution. Any credible 51% attack report = immediate sell signal for that chain. Post-attack confidence takes months to rebuild.

CRYPTO INDEX TRACKING: CCI30 tracks top 30 cryptos by market cap. If portfolio underperforms index, better off holding index. In bull markets, altcoin selection adds value. In bear markets, index with reduced size minimizes drawdowns. Rebalance monthly for market-cap-weighted momentum.

FORCE INDEX: Price Change x Volume. 2-day EMA for entries (buy dips in uptrend), 13-day EMA for trend (bulls above zero). Confirms breakout legitimacy - breakout on rising Force Index is real. Falling Force Index = likely trap. Divergence between Force Index and price = strong reversal signal.

DELTA-NEUTRAL VOLATILITY TRADING: Long straddle + dynamic delta hedging = long gamma/long vol. Profit when realized vol exceeds implied vol at entry. Crypto 24/7 markets and high realized vol create persistent gamma scalping opportunities. Requires frequent rebalancing and tight execution.

MOVING AVERAGE CROSSOVER ENTRIES: Simple MA crossover (fast crosses above slow = buy). Exponential MA responds faster. Best trend-following: 20/50 EMA crossover. Counter-trend: buy when price dips below long MA and crosses back above. All produce whipsaws in ranging markets. Crypto: 13/26 EMA on daily captures major trends.

BALANCE OF PAYMENTS REGIME: Current account deficits must be financed by capital account surpluses. If capital inflows insufficient, currency depreciates. US twin deficits (trade + budget) are structural USD headwind. In crypto: sustained ETF inflows financing trade deficit of miners selling BTC.

SEASONALITY ENTRIES: Certain markets show persistent seasonal patterns. Pure seasonality alone has weak edge, but combined with technical confirmation adds 10-20% to returns. Use as filter, not primary signal. Crypto Q4 strength, Monday gaps, month-end rebalancing.

CYCLE-BASED ENTRIES USING MESA: Maximum Entropy Spectral Analysis identifies dominant market cycles. Enter at cycle trough (buy) or peak (sell). Weakness: cycles change frequency over time. In crypto: BTC shows ~4-year dominant cycle (halving). Sub-cycles of ~6 months. Use for timing, not direction.

NEURAL NETWORK ENTRIES: Feed-forward NN trained on technical indicators predicts direction. Use small networks (3-5 nodes) to avoid overfitting. Out-of-sample testing mandatory. For crypto: train on BTC data, validate on ETH, test on SOL. Cross-asset validation prevents overfitting.

GENETIC ALGORITHM ENTRIES: Evolve rule-based entry templates. GA searches millions of combinations. Outperforms brute-force optimization. Evolved rules must make economic sense, not just fit data. Use large out-of-sample window. In crypto: evolve rules across multiple coins simultaneously for robustness.

STANDARD EXIT STRATEGY (SES): Fixed stop (3xATR) + profit target (6xATR) + time limit (10 bars). Handles all scenarios: small loss on bad trades, reasonable profit on good, forced exit on dead trades. Foundation for all exit improvements. Profited from random entries on S&P 500.

DYNAMIC ATR TRAILING STOP: Exit when price retraces N*ATR from highest high. Start wider (3xATR), tighten as trade matures. Outperforms fixed stops - adapts to volatility. In strong trends, wide stop keeps you in. Best N = 2-4 for daily bars. Never move stop further from entry.

PROFIT TARGET EXITS: Fixed target (N*ATR or N*risk) captures profits on mean-reverting moves. Testing shows: fixed targets improve win rate but reduce expectancy for trend systems. Best for short-term systems. Trend systems should use trailing stops, not fixed targets. Don't cap upside in trending regime.

CHANNEL BREAKOUT ENTRY: Buy when price exceeds N-bar highest high, sell short when below N-bar lowest low. Use limit orders at breakout level to reduce slippage. Best results: 20-55 day lookback for daily bars. Add volatility filter - only trade breakouts when ATR > N-day average ATR.

BAR-SCORING TECHNIQUE: Assign each bar score based on trend alignment, momentum, volatility, volume. Highest-scoring bars = best trade candidates. Use as filter - only take entries when bar score exceeds threshold. Outperforms single-indicator entries by capturing multi-factor confluence.

SWING ENTRY STRATEGY: Identify swing high/low (3+ bars with higher/lower close on each side). Enter on pullback to 38.2-61.8% retracement of last swing. Stop beyond swing extreme. Target: 127.2-161.8% extension. Combines price structure with Fibonacci for objective entry with defined risk.

TRAILING ONE-BAR ENTRY: After momentum and price/time targets align, enter on break of signal bar high (long) or low (short). Stop beyond opposite end of signal bar. Tight stop = favorable R:R. If stopped, re-enter on next signal bar. Works on all timeframes from 5-min to monthly.

MACD HISTOGRAM STRATEGY: MACD histogram crossing zero = momentum shift. For dual timeframe: Weekly MACD histogram rising AND daily crossing zero in same direction. Most powerful when combined with price at Fibonacci support/resistance. Simple but effective momentum filter.

DYNAMIC PRICE PROJECTION: After 5-wave trend impulse, project end of wave 5 at 61.8-100% extension of waves 1-3 combined. When wave 5 reaches zone AND momentum diverges = high probability trend completion. Take profits or tighten stops.

EXTERNAL RETRACEMENTS: When correction exceeds 100% of prior move, use external retracements (127.2%, 161.8%) to identify final correction section. These identify where correction ends and new trend begins. Critical for catching exact reversal points. Combine with time targets for maximum precision.

RANDOM ENTRY + TREND FILTER TEST: Entering randomly (coin flip) but only in direction of 350/25 EMA trend generated 32.46% CAGR with 43.74% max DD over 10 years. Best random test: 53.3% return, worst: 17.5%. Proves trend filter alone has massive edge - entry timing is secondary.

TRADER EFFECTS ON SYSTEMS: When too many traders use same breakout system, front-runners exploit known entry levels. Simple breakout systems lose edge over time. This is why broadly-parameterized, less-crowded systems are more robust. Adapt entry timing to avoid predictable levels.

OPTIMIZATION PARADOX: Choosing specific parameters (25-day vs 30-day MA) reduces predictive value of backtest itself. Solution: use broad parameter ranges that work across many values. If system works for 20-40 day breakout, more robust than one tuned to exactly 23 days.

EDGE MEASUREMENT (E-RATIO): E-ratio = MFE/MAE normalized by ATR. E70 > 1.0 = positive edge. 20-day Donchian breakout E70 = 1.20 (20% more favorable than adverse over 70 days). With trend filter, E-ratio improves to 1.35+. Use to objectively compare entry signals.

MAXIMUM ADVERSE EXCURSION (MAE): Study how far trades go against you before hitting target. Use MAE data to set stops that are wide enough to avoid noise but tight enough for good R:R. Plot MAE distribution for your system to find optimal stop distance.

TIME SERIES MOMENTUM: Buy instruments with positive N-month return, sell those with negative N-month return. Hold for 1 month. Works across futures, currencies, commodities, crypto. Return proportional to magnitude of past return. Best lookback: 12 months equities, 1-3 months crypto.

CROSS-SECTIONAL MOMENTUM: Rank all instruments by past N-month return. Buy top decile, sell bottom decile. Rebalance monthly. Long-short portfolio is market neutral. Key driver: forced asset sales/purchases (index rebalancing, margin calls, fund flows) create persistent momentum.

SCALING-IN DEBATE: Adding to losing positions in mean reversion can improve IF spread truly mean-reverts. But if regime changed, catastrophic loss. Only scale-in if half-life <5 days and cointegration robust. Better to size correctly at entry. In crypto: NEVER scale into losing positions - regime shifts are too fast.

NEWS SENTIMENT MOMENTUM: NLP-based sentiment scores from financial news predict short-term momentum. Positive = buy, negative = sell. Combine with traditional momentum. Decay is fast - hold days not weeks. Works best for large-cap with heavy coverage. In crypto: CT sentiment + on-chain metrics.

EQUITY-CRYPTO LINK: Rising US equities attract foreign capital, strengthening USD but also risk appetite. Crypto benefits from risk-on equity environment. When equities and crypto diverge, investigate - either crypto leading or about to re-correlate. BTC/SPX ratio as divergence gauge.

SIMPLE SYSTEMS WIN: Dual MA system tested with stops from 0.5-10 ATR: NO stop configuration beat the no-stop version on CAGR, MAR, Sharpe, drawdown, or drawdown duration. For trend followers, drawdowns come from giving back profits, not from entry risk. Simplicity wins over complexity.

STRADDLE/STRANGLE: Long straddle (ATM call+put) for large move either way. Max loss = premium. Best IV rank <20 with catalyst. Long strangle (OTM) cheaper, needs larger move. 30-45 DTE. Buy when IV low, sell when IV spikes. Crypto events: halvings, ETF decisions, FOMC.

SHORT STRADDLE/STRANGLE: Sell ATM call+put. Max profit = credit. Unlimited risk. IV rank >80. Close at 50% profit. Short strangle: 16-delta = ~68% PoP. Manage at 50%. Undefined risk - size strictly. Best in ranging regime with elevated IV.

BUTTERFLY: Buy 1 ITM, sell 2 ATM, buy 1 OTM. Max profit at middle strike. Profits from decay. Best for ranging markets with defined range. In crypto: use around key support/resistance levels where you expect price to pin.

BULL CALL VERTICAL: Buy lower call, sell higher. Limited risk/profit. Better than naked call - reduces cost. In crypto: use for leveraged upside exposure with defined risk. Best when IV moderate and trend confirmed.

BEAR PUT VERTICAL: Buy higher put, sell lower. Defined risk on decline. Entry when IV low. In crypto: use for hedging long positions or directional bearish bets. More capital-efficient than buying puts outright.

CALENDAR SPREAD: Sell near-term, buy longer-term same strike. Profits from decay differential and/or rising IV. Best ranging regime. In crypto: BTC calendar spreads during consolidation exploit term structure. Roll at 50% profit or 21 DTE.

COLLAR: Long stock + long put + short call. Limits both sides. Call finances put. Best = zero cost. In crypto: collar BTC holdings for downside protection while capping upside. Use when IV elevated and you want to hold but reduce risk.

BACKSPREAD: Sell 1 ATM, buy 2+ OTM. Credit or small debit. Unlimited upside. Free lottery if credit. In crypto: use before expected catalysts where you expect large move but direction uncertain. Profits from vol expansion.

CONDOR ADJUSTMENT: Tested side near strike = roll untested closer. Or close tested, keep untested free. Max loss = 2x credit. In crypto: adjust aggressively - BTC can move 20% in a day. Never let a tested iron condor ride without adjustment.

THETA DECAY TIMING: Accelerates last 30 days. ~35% value lost. Sellers target 30-45 DTE. Buyers target 60-90 DTE. In crypto: theta decay faster due to higher IV. Close/roll short options at 21 DTE minimum to avoid gamma risk.

VEGA/EVENT PLAY: IV rises before events, crushes after. Buy 21 DTE, sell 1 day before event. Average IV expansion 20-40%. In crypto: halving, ETF decisions, FOMC all create IV expansion. Buy straddle/strangle pre-event, sell pre-announcement.

IV RANK TRADING: >50 = sell premium. <30 = buy premium. 30-50 = neutral. Mispriced vol = #1 options edge. In crypto: IV has persistent premium over RV due to crash risk. Systematically selling has positive EV but catastrophic tail risk. Size for 3 sigma moves.

SCALING OUT: Exit 1/3 at 1x risk, 1/3 at 2x risk, final 1/3 with trailing stop. This locks in profits while allowing runners. Reduces emotional pressure of all-or-nothing exits. Ensures profit on most trades while allowing big trend participation.

POSITION SIZING FORMULA: Shares = (Account x Risk%) / (Entry - Stop). Never widen stops. Scale: 50% initial, 25% confirm, 25% breakout. Always calculate before entry. If ATR stop would risk >2% of account, reduce position size. Size by risk, not conviction.

RISK OF RUIN: 60% WR 1:1 R:R, risk 10% = >50% ruin probability. Risk 2% = near-zero. Kelly: (win% x avg win/avg loss) - (1-win%). With 50% WR 1:1 R:R 2% risk, probability of 50% DD = 13%. With 3% risk = 30%. With 10% = 99%. Keep effective risk <2% of total capital including leverage.

MAX CORRELATED POSITIONS: Eight highly correlated positions = one position eight times as large. A mistake in position correlation is root of serious problems. Kovner risks no more than 1% per trade and studies correlation daily. In crypto: treat all >0.7 correlated assets as one position.

STOP PLACEMENT: Place stops beyond technical barriers, not within trading ranges. Allocate max dollar risk to fewer contracts with wider stops - REVERSE of typical trader. Stop should indicate trade is wrong, not just max loss per contract. ATR-based stops (2-3x ATR) work better than fixed percentages in crypto.

THE 6% RULE: If you lose 6% of account in a calendar month, STOP TRADING for rest of month. Prevents emotional revenge trading and drawdown spirals. In crypto where you can lose 6% in hours, adapt to DAILY rule: if daily loss >3%, stop for 24 hours.

MONTHLY LOSS LIMIT: PTJ limits monthly losses: 3% stop on equity for rest of month if down. Never have a double-digit monthly loss. Reduce position size when trading poorly - trade SMALLEST position when trading is WORST. This is the single most important risk management rule.

DO NOTHING UNLESS THERE IS SOMETHING TO DO: Most people always have to be playing. They make a big play then rush to do something else. Wait until money lying in corner, then go pick it up. Trade as little as possible. Rogers. In crypto 24/7 market, doing nothing is the hardest and most profitable skill.

VARIANT PERCEPTION: Develop perceptions at variance with general market view. Play variant perceptions until no longer variant. Key: perception must be CONTRARIAN - if everyone agrees, edge is gone. Combine variant perception with predefined entry/exit for asymmetric betting.

DON'T MISS THE BIG ONES: 95% of profits from 5% of trades. Missing only a few major profit opportunities has dramatic negative impact. Guard against holding too rigid opinions. In crypto: be flexible to go long OR short any market. Direction doesn't matter - only that price trends.

NEVER AVERAGE LOSERS: Decrease trading volume when trading poorly; increase when trading well. Never trade in situations where you don't have control - don't risk significant money in front of key reports. That is gambling not trading. Averaging down on losing crypto positions = financial suicide.

OVERNIGHT POSITION RULE: When nervous about overnight/weekend position and market lets you out at much better price than expected, you are usually better off STAYING with the position. When worst fears aren't realized, consider INCREASING position. Counter-intuitive but profitable.

LIQUIDATE HALF AT EXTREMES: Always liquidate half position below new highs/lows, remaining half beyond. Floor traders buy to trigger stops at obvious levels then liquidate into them. Don't be the one getting stopped at exact high/low. Use iceberg orders for large positions.

CONTRARIAN AT EXTREMES: Markets look their very best when setting new highs - often BEST time to sell. Best money made at market turns, not by catching trends in middle. When market doesn't respond to news the way it SHOULD, it tells you something very important. Divergence = signal.

PORTFOLIO REVIEW: Steinhardt reviews portfolio SIX times daily. He is a negative monitor - only talks when things are lousy or stocks aren't acting as they should. Constant scrutiny prevents small problems from becoming large. In crypto: set alerts for key levels, check portfolio at fixed intervals, not continuously.

STOP DISTANCE vs POSITION SIZE: Most traders limit loss per contract but trade maximum contracts. REVERSE: allocate max dollar risk to fewer contracts with wider stops. Stop should indicate trade is wrong.  account, 1% risk, entry 50, stop 48 = risk /point gives 500 shares.

AVOID INTERMEDIATE TERM: Be as short-term or as long-term as you can stand. Intermediate term picks up vast majority of trend followers. Best strategy is to avoid the middle like the plague. Dennis key insight on timeframe selection. In crypto: either scalp intraday or position for multi-week trends.

MONEY MARKET ROTATION: If you make 50% two years in a row then lose 50% third year, worse than money market fund. Wait for something you know is right. Take profit, put in money market, wait again. You come out way ahead. In crypto: rotate profits to stables during uncertain periods.

BEAR MARKET RALLY TRADING: In bear markets, use sharp countertrend rallies to enter positions. Bear markets have sharp down moves followed by quick retractions unlike bull markets. In crypto: BTC bear market rallies can be 30-50%. Trade them but don't mistake for new bull. Tight stops essential.

MANAGED FUTURES ALLOCATION: Trend following has near-zero correlation to stocks/bonds. Adding 20% managed futures improves risk-adjusted returns. Biggest benefit during stock market crashes (crisis alpha). Correlation to S&P slightly negative during bear markets. In crypto: allocate to systematic trend strategies alongside discretionary.

CONSISTENCY > INTELLIGENCE: You could publish trading rules in newspaper and no one would follow them. Key is consistency and discipline. Almost anyone can make rules 80% as good as Dennis taught. What they can't do is give confidence to stick to rules when things go bad. The system is simple. Following it is hard.

THINK LIKE AN OWNER: Think of stocks as businesses not ticker symbols. Buying fractional ownership in real business with real assets and earnings. In crypto: think of tokens as ownership in protocols with real users, revenue, and TVL. This mindset eliminates most emotional trading mistakes.

DON'T PERSONALIZE MARKET: Market is totally impersonal; doesn't care whether you make money. Whenever trader says I wish or I hope, they engage in destructive thinking. Focus on diagnosis, not wishful thinking. In crypto: don't get emotionally attached to any token. The protocol doesn't know you exist.

TWO-MINUTE DRILL: Explain in 2 minutes why you own each position. If story still makes sense, hold. If story has deteriorated, sell. Never own what you cannot explain simply. If you can't explain a crypto token's value proposition in one sentence, you shouldn't own it.

PUNCH CARD APPROACH: 20 lifetime trades. Extreme selectivity. 3-5 high conviction. Quality over quantity. In crypto: there are hundreds of potential trades - be extremely selective. One great trade beats ten mediocre ones. Write down your 5 best ideas and only trade those.
